summary: Static validation of Portuguese (PT) language addition. All 16 checks passed across 3 modified files (Loc.cs, SupportedLanguagesConfig.cs, SamplingDateParser.cs). Structural integrity, key parity (998 keys matching EN exactly and in order), placeholder preservation ({0}, {0:F1}%, {LANGUAGE_NAME}), HTML tag consistency vs Italian, month-lookup uniqueness (no `set` duplicate collision with Italian), C# string escaping, brace/comma balance (IT block ends with `},` before PT, outer dict closes with `};`), and European PT conventions all verified.
critical_code_review_comments: Loc.cs is 7422 lines — exceeds the 700-line file-size guideline. Consider splitting per-language dictionaries into separate partial classes (e.g. Loc.En.cs, Loc.Pt.cs) for maintainability. Not a blocker; already known trade-off given the 3-step add-language architecture.

SamplingDateParser MonthLookup relies on intentional key omissions to avoid duplicate-key exceptions (Portuguese `set` omitted since Italian already claims it, both mapping to 9). This is documented but fragile — a future refactor could switch to a list-of-tuples with grouping to remove the hidden constraint.
context_for_next_testing_agent: PT language add: 998 keys in Loc.cs pt block match EN exactly (name+order). SupportedLanguagesConfig has 7th LangDef (pt/pt-PT/Portuguese (Português)/Português/🇵🇹/12 long+12 short months). SamplingDateParser has 15 Portuguese month tokens added at end of MonthLookup; `set` (September short) intentionally OMITTED to avoid duplicate-key with Italian `set`. IT block now terminates with `},` before PT. All 5 'arquivo' occurrences correspond to EN 'archive' keys (correct European PT — 'arquivo'=archive noun, 'ficheiro'=computer file, both used correctly, 39 'ficheiro' usages present). HTML tag additions (<code>, <em>) in CamCheckMethod2* keys match the pattern already used by RO/FR/ES/DE/IT — English is the outlier there. Only 3 source files changed vs HEAD.
